title: arenaria gypsophiloides var. viscosa
intro: Arenaria gypsophiloides var. viscosa, commonly known as sticky sandwort, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant effects. The health benefits of sticky sandwort include relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as wound healing and skin conditions like eczema. Herbal preparations of sticky sandwort typically involve infusions or decoctions of the plant's leaves and stems, which can be used to make teas, tinctures, or topical creams.
